content.text: PR-Linked Preview Environment Lifecycle

A PR-linked preview environment lives and dies with its pull request. It is created the moment a PR is opened, rebuilt on every subsequent push, and torn down automatically when the PR is merged or closed. For GenAI applications this lifecycle adds important nuances: LLM model configs, vector seeds, and prompt fixtures must all track the PR branch — not the main branch — so the preview tests what the PR actually changed.

Managing this lifecycle requires handling five distinct webhook events: opened, synchronize (new commit), closed, reopened, and a CronJob fallback for abandoned PRs that never close cleanly. Each transition has a defined sequence of operations and a verification step that confirms the environment reached the expected state before the CI job is marked green.

attrs.alt: PR-linked preview environment lifecycle state machine and event table

content.text: Full Lifecycle GitHub Actions Workflow

One workflow file handles all transitions. The `opened` and `synchronize` events share a provision/upgrade job; the `closed` event triggers teardown. A concurrency group keyed on the PR number prevents two builds from racing on rapid pushes.

# .github/workflows/preview-lifecycle.yml
name: Preview Environment Lifecycle

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed]

# Prevent two jobs for the same PR from running in parallel.
# cancel-in-progress kills a running build if a newer push arrives.
concurrency:
  group: preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event.action != 'closed' }}

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}
  IMAGE: genai-app
  CLUSTER_NAME: k8s-preview
  CLUSTER_REGION: us-east-1

jobs:
  provision-or-upgrade:
    if: github.event.action != 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
      pull-requests: write   # to post/update PR comment
      id-token: write        # for OIDC → ECR/Vault auth
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set image tag
        id: tag
        run: echo "tag=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE }}:pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}-${{ github.sha }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Build and push image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.tag }}
          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE }}:cache-pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE }}:cache-pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }},mode=max

      - name: Configure kubectl
        run: |
          aws eks update-kubeconfig --region ${{ env.CLUSTER_REGION }} --name ${{ env.CLUSTER_NAME }}

      - name: Helm install / upgrade
        id: helm
        run: |
          PR="${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          NS="preview-pr-${PR}"
          RELEASE="genai-pr-${PR}"
          EXPIRES=$(date -d "+4 hours" --utc +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

          kubectl create namespace "$NS" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          kubectl label namespace "$NS" preview=true pr="$PR" --overwrite

          helm upgrade --install "$RELEASE" ./charts/genai-preview             --namespace "$NS"             --set image.tag="${{ steps.tag.outputs.tag }}"             --set preview.prNumber="$PR"             --set preview.expiresAt="$EXPIRES"             --set preview.branch="${{ github.head_ref }}"             --wait --timeout=5m

          INGRESS_HOST=$(kubectl get ingress "$RELEASE" -n "$NS"             -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].hostname}')
          echo "url=https://${INGRESS_HOST}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Post / update PR comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const previewUrl = "${{ steps.helm.outputs.url }}";
            const prNum = context.issue.number;
            const body = [
              "## Preview Environment",
              "",
              `**URL:** ${previewUrl}`,
              `**Branch:** \`${{ github.head_ref }}\``,
              `**Built from:** \`${{ github.sha }}.substring(0,7)\``,
              `**Expires:** ~4 hours after last push (auto-extended on synchronize)`,
              "",
              "_This comment is updated on every push to this PR._",
            ].join("\n");

            const comments = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: prNum
            });
            const existing = comments.data.find(c =>
              c.user.login === "github-actions[bot]" && c.body.includes("## Preview Environment")
            );
            if (existing) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: existing.id, body
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNum, body
              });
            }

  teardown:
    if: github.event.action == 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure kubectl
        run: |
          aws eks update-kubeconfig --region ${{ env.CLUSTER_REGION }} --name ${{ env.CLUSTER_NAME }}

      - name: Helm uninstall and delete namespace
        run: |
          PR="${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          NS="preview-pr-${PR}"
          RELEASE="genai-pr-${PR}"
          helm uninstall "$RELEASE" --namespace "$NS" --wait --ignore-not-found
          kubectl delete namespace "$NS" --ignore-not-found

      - name: Clean up vector namespace
        run: python3 scripts/teardown_preview_vectors.py
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          PINECONE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.PINECONE_PREVIEW_API_KEY }}

      - name: Verify teardown
        run: python3 scripts/verify_preview_teardown.py
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          PREVIEW_DB_URL: ${{ secrets.PREVIEW_DB_URL }}
          PINECONE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.PINECONE_PREVIEW_API_KEY }}
Handling the synchronize Event Correctly

The `synchronize` event fires for every commit pushed to the PR branch, including force-pushes. The `cancel-in-progress: true` concurrency setting cancels any in-flight build when a newer commit arrives — preventing the race where commit A finishes after commit B and overwrites the preview with stale code.

The Helm `upgrade --install` command is idempotent: it upgrades an existing release or installs if none exists. This means `opened` and `synchronize` run the exact same Helm step — no branch logic needed in the workflow.

# Validate that concurrency cancellation works as expected.
# Push two commits in quick succession and confirm only the second one
# ends up as the deployed image.

# In a local terminal:
git commit --allow-empty -m "test: push 1"
git push
sleep 3
git commit --allow-empty -m "test: push 2"
git push

# Expected: the "push 1" Actions run is cancelled mid-build.
# The preview environment ends up running the image from "push 2".
# Confirm via the PR comment — SHA should match HEAD.

Force-pushes also emit synchronize, so rebase workflows and amended commits trigger a full rebuild. The cancel-in-progress guard prevents the old-SHA image from clobbering the new one if build times differ.
TTL Controller for Abandoned Environments

PRs that are abandoned without being closed leave behind running environments that consume cluster resources and accumulate costs. The TTL controller is a CronJob that scans all preview namespaces every 15 minutes and deletes any whose `preview.expiresAt` annotation has passed.

# scripts/preview_ttl_controller.py
# Run as a Kubernetes CronJob (schedule: "*/15 * * * *").
# Deletes preview namespaces whose TTL annotation has expired.

import datetime, subprocess, json, sys

def run(cmd: list[str]) -> str:
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
    return result.stdout.strip()

def get_preview_namespaces() -> list[dict]:
    raw = run(["kubectl", "get", "namespaces",
               "-l", "preview=true",
               "-o", "json"])
    return json.loads(raw)["items"]

def get_expires_at(ns: dict) -> datetime.datetime | None:
    annotations = ns.get("metadata", {}).get("annotations", {})
    raw = annotations.get("preview/expires-at")
    if not raw:
        return None
    return datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(raw.replace("Z", "+00:00"))

def teardown_namespace(name: str) -> None:
    pr = name.removeprefix("preview-pr-")
    release = f"genai-pr-{pr}"
    print(f"  TTL expired: tearing down {name} (release={release})")
    subprocess.run(
        ["helm", "uninstall", release, "--namespace", name, "--ignore-not-found", "--wait"],
        check=False,
    )
    subprocess.run(
        ["kubectl", "delete", "namespace", name, "--ignore-not-found"],
        check=False,
    )

now = datetime.datetime.now(tz=datetime.timezone.utc)
namespaces = get_preview_namespaces()
print(f"Checking {len(namespaces)} preview namespace(s) at {now.isoformat()}")

expired = 0
for ns in namespaces:
    name = ns["metadata"]["name"]
    expires_at = get_expires_at(ns)
    if expires_at is None:
        print(f"  WARN: {name} has no preview/expires-at annotation — skipping")
        continue
    remaining = (expires_at - now).total_seconds()
    if remaining <= 0:
        teardown_namespace(name)
        expired += 1
    else:
        print(f"  OK: {name} expires in {int(remaining/60)}m")

print(f"TTL pass complete: {expired} namespace(s) torn down.")

# k8s/preview-ttl-cronjob.yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: preview-ttl-controller
  namespace: preview-shared
spec:
  schedule: "*/15 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid    # skip if previous run still running
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: preview-ttl-sa  # RBAC: can list/delete namespaces + helm
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
            - name: ttl-controller
              image: ghcr.io/myorg/genai-ops:latest
              command: ["python3", "/scripts/preview_ttl_controller.py"]
              resources:
                requests: { cpu: "50m", memory: "64Mi" }
                limits: { cpu: "200m", memory: "128Mi" }
Branch-Tracking Model Config and Prompts

A preview environment must run the PR branch's prompt files and model config — not the main branch versions. The Helm chart passes the Git SHA as an environment variable; the application reads its prompt and config from a ConfigMap that the CI job patches from the PR branch before deploying.

#!/bin/bash
# Part of the provision-or-upgrade job, run before helm upgrade.
# Creates a ConfigMap with the PR branch's prompt and model-config content.

PR="${PR_NUMBER:?}"
NS="preview-pr-${PR}"

# Extract prompt content from the checked-out PR branch
PROMPT_CONTENT=$(cat prompts/system_v2/config.yaml)
MODEL_CONFIG=$(cat config/model-config.staging.yaml)

kubectl create configmap genai-preview-config   --namespace="$NS"   --from-literal=prompt_config="$PROMPT_CONTENT"   --from-literal=model_config="$MODEL_CONFIG"   --from-literal=git_sha="${GITHUB_SHA}"   --from-literal=branch="${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}"   --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

echo "ConfigMap genai-preview-config created/updated in $NS"
echo "  git_sha=${GITHUB_SHA:0:7} branch=${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}"

Mount the ConfigMap at /etc/genai/config in the Helm chart. The lru_cache means the app reads the file once at startup — if a synchronize event triggers a helm upgrade, the pod restarts and re-reads the updated ConfigMap automatically.
Posting Preview URLs as PR Comments

The CI job should post a single PR comment that is updated (not appended) on every push. Creating a new comment on each synchronize event floods the PR thread with identical entries. The pattern is: search for an existing bot comment with a known marker string, update it if found, or create a new one.
Reopened PRs

When a PR is reopened after being closed, the teardown job has already deleted the namespace, release, and vector namespace. The `reopened` event triggers the same provision path as `opened` — the Helm install creates a fresh environment from scratch. No additional handling is needed because `helm upgrade --install` is already idempotent.

# The types list in the workflow trigger covers reopened:
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed]

# The provision-or-upgrade job runs for opened, synchronize, AND reopened:
jobs:
  provision-or-upgrade:
    if: github.event.action != 'closed'   # true for opened, synchronize, reopened
Do not try to detect whether a namespace already exists and conditionally skip provisioning — the helm upgrade --install idempotency guarantee handles all three cases safely.
Summary
The lifecycle has five events: opened (provision), synchronize (upgrade), closed (teardown), reopened (re-provision), and CronJob TTL expiry (force teardown).
Use a concurrency group with cancel-in-progress: true so rapid pushes cancel in-flight builds — only the newest commit's image reaches the preview environment.
helm upgrade --install is idempotent: the same command handles both first-provision and every subsequent upgrade without branching logic in the workflow.
Create a branch-tracking ConfigMap before helm upgrade so the preview pod always runs the PR branch's prompt files and model config, not main.
Post a single PR comment with a known HTML marker string; update it on every synchronize event rather than creating a new comment each time.
Run the TTL CronJob every 15 minutes to clean up abandoned environments; use verify_preview_teardown.py as a post-close gate to confirm all resources are removed.
